rulings.Case Title: JEEBA ABBEYQUAYE VS. CHARLES BELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Depositions And Inspection Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 5. Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Depositions And Inspection Against Defendant. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The motion to compel is granted. Defendant shall produce Eric Haynes, Greg Cazatt, Troy MacDonald, Chris Gonzales, and William Judge for deposition by April 6, 2012. Defendant shall allow the inspection of its computer system which will be limited to seeking the service order and invoice referred to in the February 26, 2010 timesheet. The search shall be limited to 230 Central Avenue, San Francisco location. A protective order shall be prepared to protect the concerns related to trade secret rights, proprietary information rights, privacy rights, as well as information protected from disclosure by attorney-client or product and litigation privileges as well as a premature disclosure of expert discovery information. The search will be limited to 4 hours to minimize the burden in unreasonably interfering with the Defendant's operation of its business. Request for sanctions is Denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Anmd Otion Fopr Order Compelling Further Discovery Responses From Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS LOUIS GAMACHE AND DANIELLE MCGEE'S Notice Of Motion for Order Compelling Further Discovery Responses From Defendant. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Motion to Compel is Granted. Defendant shall amend his responses within 3 weeks as follows: 1. Mr. Ardebilchi shall provide the job titles of the individuals identified in Interrogatory No. 3, if known. 2. With respect to Interrogatories 11-13: Mr. Ardebilchi is to provide additional details of the work performed that can be obtained through information reasonably available from sources under Mr. Ardebilchi's control or state that after a reasonable and good faith effort, no other information can be provided. 3. With respect to Interrogatories 18-21, Mr. Ardebilchi shall provide more details of what needed to be done to remedy the outstanding notices of violation based on information reasonably available from sources under Mr. Ardebilchi's control or state that after a reasonable and good faith effort, no other information can be provided. Request for sanctions is Denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIM ROBINSON INDIVIDUALLY, AND WHORL LLC, VS. COOLEY GODWARD KRONISH LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTS PETER WERNER, KENNETH GUERNSEY, BARRY GRAYNOR, CRAIG JACOBY, COOLEY LLP DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth and seventh causes of action and overruled as to the first through fifth causes of action. Plaintiffs must plead fraud and negligent misrepresentation with the requisite particularity, including the individual who made the misrepresentation, what the misrepresentation was, how it was made, and when it was made. Plaintiffs' claims are not clearly and affirmatively time-barred. The first, second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action are adequately pled. rulings.Case Title: TIM ROBINSON INDIVIDUALLY, AND WHORL LLC, VS. COOLEY GODWARD KRONISH LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTS STEVE ZELINGER, GUS SPANOS, BRIAN MILLER AND JOHN MORRIS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action and overruled as to the eleventh cause of action. These Defendants are not liable for any alleged misrepresentations arising out of promises made in the Asset Purchase Agreement or Pledge Agreement or any statements made by these individuals acting on behalf of Solidus during the negotiation of these Agreements. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to plead, if they can do so in good faith, fraud or negligent misrepresentation not arising out of these Agreements. Moreover, Plaintiffs must plead fraud and negligent misrepresentation with the requisite particularity, including the individual who made the misrepresentation, what the misrepresentation was, how it was made, and when it was made. Plaintiffs' claims are not clearly and affirmatively time-barred. The eleventh cause of action is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two; Requests For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 22, PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two; Requests For Sanctions; The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest as follows: Plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant has supplemented its prior responses to the Form 17.1 Interrogatory with respect to the verified supplemental responses to requests for admissions served on January 25, 2012. The responses, with the exception to the responses to From Interrogatory 17.1 with respect to requests for admissions 18, 26, 27 and 29, as supplemented, are sufficient. Defendant shall serve supplemental responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 with respect to Requests for Admissions 18 and 26. Defendant's response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 with respect to Request for Admission 27 states a conclusion, not facts. Defendant shall supplement its response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 with respect to Request for Admission 27, stating the underlying facts supporting the conclusion in its prior response. Request for Admission 29 is ambiguous with respect to the term "accurate sales profile." Plaintiff may serve an amended Request for Admission 29 on Defendant rephrasing the question. The court adds that service of an amended Request for Admission 29 shall be served no later than March 8, 2012 by personal service, email or fax. In the event Plaintiff does so, Defendant shall respond to Form Interrogatory 17.1 with respect to Request for Admission No. 29. The court adds that all further responses shall be served no later than March 17, 2012. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One; Requests For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 22, PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One; Requests For Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest as follows: Motion granted in part and denied in part. Defendant has supplemented its prior responses to the requests for admissions with verified supplemental responses served on January 25, 2012. The responses, with the exception to the responses to RFA No. 18, 26, and 29, as supplemented, are sufficient. Defendant continues to object, without responding, to Request for Admissions Numbers 18, 26, and 29. Defendant's objections to RFA numbers 18 and 26 are not well taken. The requests for admissions are not so vague and ambiguous as to preclude an intelligent reply. Defendant shall serve substantive responses to Requests for Admissions 18 and 26. Request for Admission 29 is ambiguous with respect to the term "accurate sales profile." Plaintiff may serve an amended Request for Admission 29 on Defendant rephrasing the question. The court adds that service of an amended Request for Admission 29 shall be served no later than March 8, 2012 by personal service, email or fax. In the event Plaintiff does so, Defendant shall respond to Request for Admission No. 29. The court adds that all further responses shall be served no later than March 17, 2012. The requests of the parties for monetary sanctions against each other is denied. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two; Requests For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 22, PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two; Requests For Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest as follows: Granted. Any privileged documents shall be identified in appropriate privilege logs. ESI searches to be conducted at the same time and under the same protocol as for the Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production, Set One. The time frame for any products shall also be the same as measured by the product life referenced in aforementioned motion. Sanctions of $ 400.00 against the defendant are warranted. The court adds a due date of April 2, 2012 for payment. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $14,700
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 22, PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $14,700, The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest as follows: Denied as to request numbers 62-66. Granted as to request numbers 68 and 69. To the extent that any responsive documents are privileged under attorney-client privilege, HIPAA, or other, defendant must identify such documents in a privilege log and disclose the existence of the documents. Further, to the extent that any trade secret or patent privilege is asserted, production is warranted subject to a protective order to maintain confidentiality of trade secrets. Any such privileged documents subject to a trade secret privilege must also be reflected in a privilege log. Granted as to Requests 1-20 and 4-70, except for 62-66, to the extent that further documents exist as determinated by the market life of the product, "Reverse." The logical time frame is defined by the product's lifespan. If the existing product is still for sale to the public for the same use (skin lightening), under the name "Reverse" or a different name, then the time frame for discovery shall be defined by the market life of the product. This could mean the time frame extends to the date of the request ("to present") if the product is still on the market. Alternatively, if defendant discontinued the sale or manufacture of the product for the same use as plaintiff's use, then the responses shall be limited according to the discontinuation date of the product. ESI search (both as to Set One and Set Two) shall be conducted in two stages. (1) During the first stage, the search will be confined to the 12 ESI Custodians proposed by defendant. The devices searched shall be limited to the custodians' laptop computers, desktop computers, and the "S" Drive. Mobile devices which are typically sync'd to the computers or the S Drive are not included in the first stage search. Custodian's PDA's and cell phones which contain data unique to those devices may be searched. However, if defendant certifies that those mobile devices do not contain unique information, then the devices are not included in the search. The ESI search terms attached as Exhibit A to the Declaration of Ian Bagger shall be supplemented to include (a) the specific search string modifications requested by plaintiff (see Surreply to Opposition, filed Feb. 7, 2012, page 5); (b) the terms, "HQ" where the term "hydroquinone" is included; and (c) the words, "harm," "danger", and "risk" added to the search terms related to safety in response to numbers 11, 12, 42 and 56. (2) If, following production from the first stage search, additional material witnesses are identified who possess responsive ESI, then a second stage search may be made of devices used by those additional witnesses. The same search terms would apply to those additional searches. On all ESI searches, privilege logs and clawback procedures will be in effect to protect against inadvertent disclosure of privileged information. Any inadvertent disclosure of confidential information is automatically subject to the terms of any protective order. Sanctions of $800 against the defendant are warranted. The court adds a due date of April 2, 2012 for payment. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC. VS. CAMMIE MCDANIEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award; Memo P/A; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 1, 2012, Line 16, PETITIONER ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC. Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award IS Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
